texto: I picture the vast realm of the sciences as an immense landscape scattered with patches of dark and light. The goal towards which we must work is either to extend the boundaries of the patches of light, or to increase their number. One of these tasks falls to the creative genius; the other requires a sort of sagacity combined with perfectionism.

texto: You live as if you were destined to live forever, no thought of your frailty ever enters your head, of how much time has already gone by you take no heed. You squander time as if you drew from a full and abundant supply, so all the while that day which you bestow on some person or thing is perhaps your last. You have all the fears of mortals and all the desires of immortals… What foolish forgetfulness of mortality to defer wise resolutions to the fiftieth or sixtieth year, and to intend to begin life at a point to which few have attained.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Denis Diderot: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. texto: Isn't it better to have men be ungrateful than to miss a chance to do good?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Denis Diderot que provoca temas ligados a gratidao. Ao questionar “Isn't it better to have men be ungrateful than to miss a chance to do good?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre gratidao, helping, people, com foco em people.

texto: One swallows the lie that flatters, but sips the bitter truth drop by drop.

texto: Power acquired by violence is only a usurpation, and lasts only as long as the force of him who commands prevails over that of those who obey.

texto: É tão arriscado acreditar em tudo como não acreditar em nada.
explicacao: De Denis Diderot, filósofo do Iluminismo francês. Alerta para dois extremos igualmente perigosos: crer em tudo cegamente ou não confiar em nada, negando juízo crítico.
